awakeWellModulated.text: When awake, the background consisted of well developed, well-modulated ______ Hz activity with posterior predominance that reacted to eye opening and eye closure.

awakeModulated.text: When awake, the background consisted of ______ Hz activity with posterior predominance that reacted to eye opening and eye closure.

noNormalBackground.text: There is no normal background activity.

posteriorBackgroundNotAssessed.text: The posterior background during full wakefulness can not be assessed.

maximumAlertBackground.text: When maximally alert, the background consisted of ______

severelySuppressed.text: The background was severely suppressed, with no identification of any cerebralactivity at sensitivity of 70 microV/cm.

brainDeathProtocolResult.text: Using the brain death protocol with double distance derivations, high sensitivity at 20 microV/cm and high cut filter set at 35 Hz and after testing the integrity of the system by taping each electrode separately and stimulating the patient, ______.

brainDeathProtocolNegative.short: Brain death protocol - no activity detected
brainDeathProtocolNegative.text: Using the brain death protocol with double distance derivations, high sensitivity at 20 microV/cm and high cut filter set at 35 Hz and after testing the integrity of the system by taping each electrode separately and stimulating the patient, no electrocerebral activity above noise level was detected.

variabilityAndReactivity.text: The background activity showed evidence of spontaneous variability and reactivity to external stimuli.

variabilityNoReactivity.text: The background activity showed evidence of spontaneous variability. Reactivity to external stimuli was not tested.

noVariabilityNoReactivity.text: The background activity did not show evidence of spontaneous varibility or reactivity to external stimuli.

noVariabilityReactivityNotTested.text: The background activity did not show evidence of spontaneous varibility. Reactivity to external stimuli was not tested.

anteriorLowAmplitudeBeta.text: Anteriorly, low amplitude beta activity was seen.

excessiveBetaDiffuse.text: Excessive beta activity was seen diffusely over both hemispheres.

betaPaucity.text: There is a paucity of beta activity over ${EDITME}.

frontocentralDrowsiness.text: During stage II sleep, sleep spindles and vertex waves were seen over the frontocentral derivations.

parasagittalDrowsiness.text: During stage II sleep, paucity of sleep spindles and vertex waves were seen over the (bilateral/right/left) parasagittal region.

asynchronousDrowsiness.text: During stage II sleep, sleep spindles and vertex waves were asynchronous but overall symmetrical.

drowsinessNotRecorded.text: Stage II sleep was not recorded.

drowsinesSlowing.text: During drowsiness, generalized slowing of the background was seen.

sleepSlowing.text: During behavioral sleep, further generalized slowing of the background activity was seen.

sleepSlowingWithSuppression.text: During behavioral sleep, further generalized slowing of the background activity was seen with a superimposed intermittent suppression.

sleepSlowingWithAttenuation.text: During behavioral sleep, further generalized slowing and attenuation of the background activity was seen.

abnormalSleepArchitecture.text: There was an absence of normal sleep architecture features.

activation.text: resulted in activation of the abovementioned epileptiform discharges.

noSeizures.text: No clinical or electrographic seizures were recorded.

focalSharpWaves.text: {${EDITME}: Frequent/occasional/rare} focal sharp waves originating from the ${EDITME}.

focalSharpWavesDetailed.text: ${EDITME}, focal spikes / sharp waves / spike and slow wave / sharp waves with an aftergoing slow wave / polyspikes / rounded contour sharp waves/ ${EDITME} were seen with a maximum potential over the ${EDITME} electrode site and a field involving the ${EDITME}.

focalSlowingDetailed.text: ${EDITME}, focal ${EDITME} slowing was seen with a maximum potential over the ${EDITME} electrode site and a field involving the ${EDITME}.

focalSlowingMax.text: ${EDITME}, focal slowing was seen with a similar topographic distribution.

focalSlowingExcessive.text: The focal slowing is considered excessive for age.

roundedContourSharpWaves.text: Rounded contour sharp waves were seen originating independently from ${EDITME} areas. These discharges were {0} in wakefulness and increased in abundance in drowsiness and sleep to become {0}.

generalizedDischargesWithSpikes.text: During those states, fragments of the generalized discharges were seen over one of the ${EDITME} areas with a shifting hemispheric emphasis, as well as a brief bursts of generalized spike and polyspike and wave discharges lasting up to ${EDITME} seconds and not associated with any detectable clinical change. Those discharges typically overrode a K-complex or a sleep spindle (dyshormia pattern). The frequency of the epileptiform discharges during wakefulness and during stage II sleep was rated as ${EDITME}.

generalizedDischargesFragmented.text: During those states, fragments of the generalized discharges were seen over one of the ${EDITME} areas with a shifting hemispheric emphasis. The frequency of the epileptiform discharges during wakefulness and during stage II sleep was rated as ${EDITME}.

spiky Alpha.short: Spiky alpha activity on eye closure
spiky Alpha.text: The salient feature of this recording was the frequent occurrence after eye closure of spiky alpha activity seen over both posterior head regions, ${EDITME} with either eye flutter or eyelid myoclonia.

breachRhythm.text: Breach rhythm was seen over the ${EDITME}.

breachRhythmDetailed.text: Breach rhythm manifesting as ${EDITME} was ${EDITME} seen localized to the ${EDITME}.

artifactContamination.text: To note, this recording was abundantly contaminated with artifacts.

recordedSeizure.text: The patient habitual spell of ${EDITME} was video-recorded at ${EDITME}. On scalp-EEG, no definite detectable change was noted.

generalizedSlowing.text: ${EDITME} generalized slowing of the background activity.

intermittentSuppression.text: Intermittent suppression of the background activity.

profoundSuppression.text: Profound suppression of the background activity.

normalFeatureAbsence.text: Absence of normal sleep architecture features.

atypicalSpikeWaveBursts.short: Bursts of atypical spike and wave discharges
atypicalSpikeWaveBursts.text: ${EDITME} bursts of generalized atypical spike and polyspike and wave discharges seen during ${EDITME}.

generalizedSpikeWaveFragments.short: Fragments of generalized spike and wave
generalizedSpikeWaveFragments.text: ${EDITME} fragments of generalized spike and wave discharges seen during ${EDITME}.

photoparoxysmalResponse.text: ${EDITME} photoparoxysmal responses following stimulation with multiple flash frequencies.

spiky AlphaNoMyoclonia.text: Spiky alpha following eye closure not associated with eyelid flutter or eyelid myoclonia.

spiky AlphaWithMyoclonia.text: Spiky alpha following eye closure associated with eyelid flutter or eyelid myoclonia.

generalizedSlowSpikeWave.text: Generalized slow spike and wave discharges.

excessiveBetaActivity.text: Excessive beta activity seen diffusely over both hemispheres.

lrda.text: LRDA seen over the (left/right) temporal head region.

1.text: a localization-related epilepsy, with focal irritative zone involving the ${EDITME} head regions.

6.text: an idiopathic/genetic generalized epilepsy and are compatible with the patient previous diagnosis of ${EDITME}.

normalRecordFull.text: This is a normal record in wakefulness, drowsiness and sleep. No focal slowing or epileptiform discharges were seen. No clinical or electrographic seizures were recorded.

normalRecordPartial.text: This is a normal record in wakefulness and drowsiness states. No focal slowing or epileptiform discharges were seen. No clinical or electrographic seizures were recorded.

diffuseDisturbance.text: This record is indicative of ${EDITME} diffuse disturbance in cerebral function of non-specific etiology.

diffuseWithFocalDysfunction.short: Diffuse disturbance with superimposed focal dysfunction
diffuseWithFocalDysfunction.text: This record is indicative of ${EDITME} diffuse disturbance in cerebral function of non-specific etiology with a superimposed ${EDITME} neuronal dysfunction over the ${EDITME}.

focalNeuronalDysfunction.text: This record is indicative of ${EDITME} neuronal dysfunction involving the ${EDITME}.

structuralLesionRuleOut.short: Structural lesion to be ruled out
structuralLesionRuleOut.text: A structural lesion involving that region needs to be ruled out.

epileptiformDischargeType.text: The epileptiform discharges seen in this record are of the type seen in patients with {0}.

generalizedDischargeType.text: In addition, the generalized discharges are of the type seen in patients with {0}.

benignMaturationalEpilepsy.text: The morphology of the discharges, their topographic distribution and pattern of activation are typical of those seen in patients with a benign maturational epilepsy.

eegTraitInheritance.text: This pattern can, however, be inherited as an EEG trait without association with seizures.

noFocalSlowingOrDischarges.text: No focal slowing or epileptiform discharges were seen.

noFocalSlowingOrSeizures.text: No focal slowing or seizures were recorded.

noSeizures.text: No clinical or electrographic seizures were recorded.

nonEpilepticSpell.text: The recorded spell is diagnostic of non-epileptic ${EDITME} spell.

nonEpilepticSpells.text: The recorded spells were non-epileptic in nature.

electrocerebralSilenceCannotBeAscertaine.text: In addition, electrocerebral silence cannot be ascertained.

electrocerebralSilence.text: This record is diagnostic of electrocerebral silence.

grdaTriphasic.text: GRDA with triphasic morphology are usually seen in the setting of a metabolic, toxic or infectious process.

grdaNonspecific.text: Although, GRDA with frontal predominace has been described in metabolic encephalopathy, this pattern is non-specific in nature.

lrda.text: LRDA of temporal origin is considered epileptiform in nature.

seizureDiagnostic.text: The recorded seizure is diagnostic of ${EDITME}.

breachRhythmExplanation.text: The breach rhythm is a reflection of the patient's previous craniotomy.

excessiveBetaExplanation.text: The excessive beta activity is a reflection of the benzodiazepine intake.

normalVariant.text: ${EDITME} are considered normal variant of unknown significance and not epileptiform in nature.
